claim: Credit and equity market composition has structurally decoupled: top 10 S&P 500 hold ~$500B net cash with zero Merton linkage, while HY issuers are $2-5B market-cap leveraged companies.
needs: Analysis of demand channel strength (mega-cap customers need credit even if mega-caps don't), testing whether HY signals predict Russell 2000 vs S&P 500 differentially, assessment of permanence of current tech mega-cap dominance.

claim: Multi-step contagion chains complete only ~15-25% of the time (median ~38%), with policy intervention as primary chain-breaker.
needs: Larger sample than 6 episodes for statistical inference, formal probability modeling of chain completion, assessment of current policy space constraints (inflation blocking Fed response).

claim: Private credit spillover operates through Godley sectoral balance (flow-of-funds) disruption rather than price contagion, transmitting chronically over quarters-to-years.
needs: Empirical testing of the Godley framework applied to private credit, integration with the acute portfolio balance channel as complementary time horizons of same phenomenon, quantification of the chronic flow-withdrawal effect.

claim: The credit impulse-to-equity R-squared has experienced a structural break from 0.38 to 0.12, reflecting genuine post-GFC funding shift rather than extended lag structure.
needs: Variable-lag VAR specifications over longer windows, decomposition into bank vs non-bank credit components, testing whether the impulse still predicts macro recessions even if degraded for S&P 500 returns.

claim: The Kalecki fiscal buffer raising the HY threshold by 100-150bp is itself destabilizing because more credit damage accumulates before equities register the problem — analogous to suppressing small forest fires.
needs: Empirical calibration of the higher pain threshold, corroboration from additional agents, historical precedent for fiscal-buffer-masked credit deterioration and eventual adjustment magnitude.

claim: A yield-curve-mediated contagion sequence operates as: inversion → bear steepener → credit blowout → equity drawdown.
needs: More than n=3 recession episodes for sequence validation. Bear steepener as uniquely dangerous risk factor retains independent value even without full sequence confirmation.

claim: The 5-year 5-year forward rate above 4.5% acts as a joint constraint on both maturity wall refinancing costs and equity multiples simultaneously.
needs: Testing the specific 4.5% threshold for structural breaks from QE era, empirical validation of 5y5y-equity relationship stability, assessment of whether the level is approximate or precisely calibrated.
